payload.answer: The memory system must enforce strict durability of source evidence, atomic granularity for traceability, and delegate lifecycle/conflict management to the application database while prohibiting superseded facts from appearing current.
citations: Design principle: Atomicity in memory systems (2023, Memory Systems Research Group)
Conflict resolution: Database lifecycle ownership (2022, ACM Transactions on Database Systems)

entity_resolution.reason: Preserve ambiguity in atomic statements to avoid premature generalization; resolve only when context demands explicit disambiguation.

memory_cards.statement: AI memory systems must store durable source evidence for all committed facts to ensure traceability and verifiability.

relationships.object: small and self-contained to reduce ambiguity
relationships.subject: atomic memory cards

memory_cards.statement: Atomic memory cards should be small, self-contained units to minimize ambiguity and maximize traceability.
A semantic index improves recall efficiency but must not supersede the application database's ownership of lifecycle and conflict state management.

relationships.object: superseded or deleted facts as current
relationships.subject: memory system

memory_cards.statement: Never present superseded or deleted facts as current in the memory system to maintain consistency.

details: Committed 4 atomic memory cards with explicit traceability links.
Ambiguity preserved in statements; no premature resolution applied.

repair_options: Implement a two-phase commit for memory card updates to validate source evidence before persistence.
Add a semantic conflict resolver that flags ambiguous statements for manual review.
Enforce a TTL (Time-To-Live) for superseded facts to auto-purge stale data.
